jokes.text: Oh, look at me! The millionaire who goes to see doctors!
jokes.setup: Someone suggests getting a doctor to remove Charlie's cast
jokes.punchline: Dennis sarcastically implies he's too poor for medical care while running a bar
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dennis's vanity and delusion about his status. Well-structured sarcasm with good economy.

jokes.text: You're not part of the gang either, Dee, so forget that. / Yes, I am. / You're not. The gang is me, Mac and Charlie. That's the gang.
jokes.setup: Dee wants to be part of the gang
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately excludes his own sister with casual cruelty
jokes.explanation: Establishes the show's dynamic of casual cruelty between siblings. Good character work but fairly standard for the show.

jokes.text: Well, that's a tough situation you got over there. / You got that whole tsunami... / No? / Well, the Superdome thing that... / Charlie. / No, there's no Superdome. / It's one of those places over there.
jokes.setup: Ari mentions moving from Israel due to danger
jokes.punchline: Charlie confuses Israel with various unrelated disasters and locations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - his complete ignorance about world events delivered with confidence. Great character integration.

jokes.text: You can't just come in here and steal our land from us. / I'm... I'm pretty sure that's how this country was founded in the first place.
jokes.setup: Dennis complains about someone taking their land
jokes.punchline: Ari points out the irony given American history
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor that highlights American hypocrisy. Perfect setup/punchline structure with excellent economy.

jokes.text: A grilled Charlie has peanut butter last. Okay, either inside or outside. Peanut butter outside. Chocolate inside. Butter inside. Cheese outside.
jokes.setup: Making what appears to be a grilled sandwich
jokes.punchline: Charlie's grotesque sandwich recipe with impossible ingredient placement
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie absurdist moment. The 'grilled Charlie' becomes a recurring show element. High originality and character integration.

jokes.text: Where are your balls, dude? Where are your balls? You're a grown man. Think about your balls. Where are your balls? / Charlie, don't talk about my balls. / I can't even mention your balls around you.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to motivate Frank to face his wife
jokes.punchline: Absurd repetition of 'balls' and Frank's discomfort with the word
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor elevated by the escalation and Frank's genuine discomfort. Good structure but relies on repetition.

jokes.text: Jesus Christ, Frank. This place is a shit hole! Is this how you've been living?
jokes.setup: Barbara enters Frank's new living situation
jokes.punchline: Her immediate horrified reaction to the squalor
jokes.explanation: Standard rich person shocked by poor conditions. Works but not particularly original.

jokes.text: While you were out making money, who do you think was at home cooking and cleaning and raising your children? / A series of Mexican women.
jokes.setup: Barbara's emotional appeal about sacrificing for the family
jokes.punchline: Frank's blunt revelation that hired help did everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. Completely deflates Barbara's emotional manipulation with brutal honesty. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Burn. There you go, buddy. / Unbelievable, dude. You're on fire. / Beautiful.
jokes.setup: Frank delivering harsh truths to Barbara
jokes.punchline: Charlie acting like Frank's hype man during the argument
jokes.explanation: Charlie treating a marital argument like entertainment. Great character moment showing his complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: Maybe you should have somebody deported like you used to in the old days. / Beautiful.
jokes.setup: Discussion about missing items from their home
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual reference to past deportations, Charlie's approval
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about Frank's past delivered casually. Shows the moral bankruptcy of both characters.

jokes.text: We don't have any... legal recourse. / No. / All right. I think I follow, fellas. / All right. We don't have any... Legal recourse. / What you're saying is, uh, we got to get creative.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explains they have no legal options
jokes.punchline: The gang immediately interprets this as advice to break the law
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how the gang twists innocent advice into criminal planning. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: No, of course we're mistaken. No, no, no. Yeah, no. We're mistaken. No, we would never want to implicate certain parties...
jokes.setup: The lawyer clarifies he's not suggesting anything illegal
jokes.punchline: Dennis and Mac's increasingly obvious attempts to get criminal advice while pretending innocence
jokes.explanation: Funny but somewhat repetitive escalation of their obvious criminal intent.

jokes.text: Jewish, okay? He's Jewish. Don't use the word 'Jew.' / I don't appreciate that. / That is a racial slur, Charlie, and... / Calling somebody a Jew who is a Jew is not a racial slur.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being anti-Semitic
jokes.punchline: The gang's confused and offensive attempt to be politically correct
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor about the gang's ignorance. Works for character development but the topic makes it uncomfortable rather than funny.

jokes.text: You're the most misinformed person I've ever met. You don't even know what's going on in Israel.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dennis argue about terminology
jokes.punchline: Dennis claims Charlie is misinformed while being completely wrong himself
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Dennis is about to demonstrate his own complete ignorance.

jokes.text: Calling somebody a Jew who is a Jew is not a racial slur... It's about the context. If you use the right context... you're good.
jokes.setup: Debate about appropriate terminology
jokes.punchline: Charlie and Mac's confident but wrong understanding of language and prejudice
jokes.explanation: Shows the gang's confident ignorance about sensitive topics.

jokes.text: Well, okay. There's a war on terror, Charlie. / Have you ever seen CNN? / Okay. George Bush right now... / Well, start from the beginning. / Saddam Hussein has the oil. / We have decided to help the people of Israel... To give our oil back to us... By defeating Saddam Hussein.
jokes.setup: Mac claims to understand Middle East politics
jokes.punchline: His completely garbled explanation that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac moment - confident ignorance about complex topics. Shows character perfectly while satirizing political ignorance.

jokes.text: My head is, like, swelling with knowledge. / With wonderful knowledge.
jokes.setup: Mac's terrible explanation of Middle East politics
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sarcastic response pretending to be enlightened
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response that shows Charlie can occasionally be the voice of reason. Nice economy and delivery.

jokes.text: I assume you did something stupid.
jokes.setup: Barbara mentions being in a neck brace
jokes.punchline: Dee's casual assumption her mother caused her own injury
jokes.explanation: Shows the family's dysfunctional dynamics. Good economy but fairly predictable for the show's style.

jokes.text: Dennis ran over me with his car. / There you go again! Don't you think it's about time you start taking responsibility for your actions?
jokes.setup: Barbara explains how she got injured
jokes.punchline: She immediately blames the victim (herself) for being run over
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Barbara's complete lack of self-awareness. The absurdity of blaming yourself for being hit by a car is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Because they stamp it out and they get poop all over their shoes. Poop on their shoes. Their shoes, dude.
jokes.setup: Mac asks what throwing a flaming bag of poop will accomplish
jokes.punchline: Charlie's overly excited explanation of the simple prank
jokes.explanation: Charlie's childlike excitement over a juvenile prank fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Let's throw a flaming bag of poop in the window. / What? Why? / Because they stamp it out and they get poop all over their shoes. / What in the hell is that going to accomplish? / Poop on their shoes. Their shoes, dude.
jokes.setup: Need to take action against Ari
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate explanation of the classic prank with total conviction
jokes.explanation: Charlie treating a juvenile prank like a sophisticated plan. Good character work but the prank itself is clichéd.

jokes.text: This Jew's in for a ton of work. / Oh! / Whoa. What? / Come on, man. You can't say things like that. / What did I say? / You dropped a hard 'J' on us.
jokes.setup: Mac talking about toilet paper cleanup
jokes.punchline: The gang treating 'Jew' like an offensive slur when Mac uses it casually
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag about the gang's confusion over what's offensive. 'Hard J' is a clever term.

jokes.text: This Jew's in for a ton of work. Oh! Whoa. What? You dropped a hard 'J' on us.
jokes.setup: Charlie refers to Ari cleaning toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis act like 'Jew' is an offensive slur
jokes.explanation: Great callback to their earlier confusion, 'hard J' is particularly funny terminology.

jokes.text: What is that, musk? / That's my new friend. I met him outside of that disgusting bar our children own.
jokes.setup: Frank notices something different about Barbara
jokes.punchline: Barbara's casual mention of her new male friend while insulting their kids
jokes.explanation: Sets up the relationship drama while showing Barbara's pettiness. Functional but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Are you bangin' him? You bangin' this guy? / Not yet, but I'm giving it serious consideration.
jokes.setup: Frank's crude question about Barbara's relationship
jokes.punchline: Barbara's matter-of-fact response about considering adultery
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Barbara's unexpected directness. Nice economy and timing.

jokes.text: He used to beat me, you know.
jokes.setup: Frank's aggressive behavior toward Ari
jokes.punchline: Barbara's casual revelation about domestic abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. Shows Barbara's manipulation tactics while revealing family dysfunction.

jokes.text: That teeny, tiny little dog.
jokes.setup: Frank asks what Barbara loves most
jokes.punchline: The dismissive description of Barbara's beloved small dog
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dog theft plot while showing Frank's disdain.

jokes.text: What are you doing with your hands? What does that mean? / Dog. Kitchen. / That's 'kitchen'? Why can't you go get the dog? / Because I got a cast on my foot. / I got a neck brace.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to communicate through gestures
jokes.punchline: Absurd sign language and escalating excuses about injuries
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with the absurdity of both being too injured for simple tasks. Good visual element.

jokes.text: Visual: Dennis doing elaborate hand gestures to communicate 'dog' and 'kitchen' to Dee
jokes.setup: Need for silent communication during break-in
jokes.punchline: Dennis's overcomplicated charades for simple concepts
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy showing Dennis making simple theft unnecessarily theatrical.

jokes.text: These eggs are organic. They're, like, three times the size of normal eggs and twice the price. If this doesn't show this guy we mean business, nothing's gonna.
jokes.setup: Mac presents his plan to intimidate Ari
jokes.punchline: Expensive organic eggs as a threat, delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - overthinking a simple prank with consumer consciousness. The earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What? Where does... What the hell is this? He put up a fence. / He didn't notice the toilet paper? He put up a fence. / He walled us in! / He walled us in!
jokes.setup: Expecting to see reaction to their toilet paper prank
jokes.punchline: Ari escalated dramatically by building a fence, making their prank irrelevant
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows the gang's petty pranks are no match for actual power. Visual element works well.

jokes.text: Visual: Gang discovers Ari has walled them into their own bar with a fence
jokes.setup: Gang arrives to throw eggs
jokes.punchline: They're trapped by Ari's countermove
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual escalation showing Ari is smarter than the gang.

jokes.text: Squeeze through there. / It gets thinner, Dennis! Wait! Don't push me! / Wait, wait, wait! My toe's caught in the fence! / Go back! / That's fine. Just go back. Just go back. / I can't move!
jokes.setup: Trying to squeeze through the fence
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic struggle as they get stuck
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy sequence. Works for the situation but nothing particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: You haven't thought it through? It destroyed my apartment. / Look. We're living in the moment. We're running around stealing things. / We're doing anything we want.
jokes.setup: Dee questions Frank's plan with the dog
jokes.punchline: Frank romanticizing their criminal behavior as spontaneous living
jokes.explanation: Frank's midlife crisis manifesting as crime spree romanticization. Good character work showing his delusion.

jokes.text: So you've been in here tearing apart pillows and... pooping on the floor? / Yes.
jokes.setup: Ari trying to understand the chaos in Frank's apartment
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact admission to bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Frank's casual admission to animalistic behavior. Great economy and delivery.

jokes.text: Cut, cut, cut, cut! What the hell are you doing, dude? That's what those tapes sound like.
jokes.setup: They're filming a terrorist threat video
jokes.punchline: Mac's 'terrorist voice' sounds nothing like actual terrorists
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy about their amateur terrorist video production.

jokes.text: That's what those tapes sound like. / Why don't you read the script I wrote? / I'm not reading the script. It's in English and riddled with spelling errors.
jokes.setup: Making a terrorist video
jokes.punchline: Dennis's critique of Mac's script quality while making terrorism threats
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dennis's vanity about quality even in criminal activity. Meta humor about script writing.

jokes.text: Why don't you head down to the Wawa and pick up a machine gun?
jokes.setup: Mac wants a weapon for the terrorist video
jokes.punchline: Charlie suggests buying a machine gun at a convenience store
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie absurdism - treating Wawa like it sells military weapons. Very quotable Philadelphia reference.

jokes.text: Why don't you head down to the Wawa and pick up a machine gun?
jokes.setup: Mac wants weapons for the video
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sarcastic suggestion to buy military weapons at a convenience store
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist line that deflates Mac's action movie fantasies with Philadelphia reality.

jokes.text: I feel like I should have something in my hands. / You don't need anything in your hands. / Like a weapon. / A machete or a machine gun. / Why don't you head down to the Wawa and pick up a machine gun?
jokes.setup: Mac wanting props for the terrorist video
jokes.punchline: Dennis's sarcastic suggestion about buying weapons at a convenience store
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Mac's impracticality and Dennis's exasperation. Wawa reference adds local flavor.

jokes.text: You not make restaurant here. Bad decision. / We will crush your skull with our weapons!
jokes.setup: Filming threatening video
jokes.punchline: Terrible fake accents and over-the-top threats
jokes.explanation: Cringey racist accents that work for character development but the humor is cheap. Shows their ignorance effectively.

jokes.text: We can't send this. / Nope. / What are you guys talking about? / We're gonna get arrested. / Yeah, or murdered. This is a little too extreme, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Watching their completed terrorist video
jokes.punchline: Sudden realization of the actual consequences of their actions
jokes.explanation: Good moment of reality hitting them. Shows how their schemes always spiral beyond their comprehension.

jokes.text: I have better aim than you. I should throw the bag of poop. / Dude, I sat over the bag for 20 minutes. I'm throwing it.
jokes.setup: Deciding who throws the poop bag
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dedication to the poop preparation process
jokes.explanation: Charlie's commitment to gross tasks is perfect character work. The 20-minute detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I have better aim than you. I should throw the bag of poop. Dude, I sat over the bag for 20 minutes. I'm throwing it.
jokes.setup: Debate over who throws the poop
jokes.punchline: Charlie's claim that preparation time gives him throwing rights
jokes.explanation: Charlie's logic that defecation time grants ownership rights is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Uh, I think this is the wrong message. / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Their poop bag thrown into empty building
jokes.punchline: Realizing the building exploded/burned down from their actions
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction to massive escalation. The casualness of their response to destruction is brilliantly written.

jokes.text: Uh, I think this is the wrong message. Yeah.
jokes.setup: They throw the flaming bag and it presumably starts a fire
jokes.punchline: Their prank has escalated into arson
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from juvenile prank to serious crime. The understatement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I'm part of the gang... or I send your asses to jail. / I would rather go to jail than work with you. / Yes! / Just for the record, I would rather not spend my life in jail.
jokes.setup: Frank's ultimatum about joining the gang
jokes.punchline: Dennis choosing jail over Frank, Mac immediately backing down
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dennis's stubbornness vs Mac's cowardice. Good escalation and contrast.

jokes.text: The building you blew up. The building you set on fire. What'd you think, I wasn't gonna find out about that?
jokes.setup: Ari reveals he knows about their sabotage
jokes.punchline: Their 'prank' was actually arson and he traced it back to them
jokes.explanation: Great revelation that their incompetent prank had serious consequences.

jokes.text: Frank, congratulations. You're captain of the gang. / No! Charlie... / Charlie, shut your mouth! / Why can't you just die and leave your money to your kids like normal parents of America?
jokes.setup: Frank becoming gang leader
jokes.punchline: Dee's blunt wish for her father's death for inheritance
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and perfectly captures the family's dysfunction. The casual tone makes the horrible sentiment funnier.

jokes.text: Frank, congratulations. You're captain of the gang. No! Charlie... Charlie, shut your mouth!
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to make Frank gang captain instead
jokes.punchline: Mac's desperate attempt to stop Dennis from making things worse
jokes.explanation: Good panic response showing how everything is falling apart.

jokes.text: You gentlemen know anything about this? Uh... Oh... shit.
jokes.setup: Police show them evidence (presumably the terrorist video)
jokes.punchline: Their stunned realization they've been caught
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that brings all their terrible decisions full circle. The silence before 'shit' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Oh... shit.
jokes.setup: Police showing them evidence of the building destruction
jokes.punchline: Mac's understated reaction to being caught for a major crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated ending. The simple reaction to massive consequences is perfectly timed. Great economy.
